mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns as capital flows into risk assets.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations collapse.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes above the 0.5pp threshold, or the signal's Sharpe ratio turns negative over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM moves in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity utilization approaching maximums, which compresses profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases consistently precede positive 5-day returns, or the mechanism's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in a walk-forward test across three consecutive late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and corporate hiring, which supports earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) over a 5-year rolling window, or the signal generates negative alpha in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 240.0 indicates entrenched inflation that forces aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.0, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals corporate sector weakness despite headline growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with a hit rate below 45% across 100 historical observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate during a post-shock recovery phase reflects temporary labor market normalization rather than sustainable growth, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines during post-shock periods consistently precede positive 5-day returns, or the mechanism's predictive power (Rank IC) remains below 0.02 across all identified recovery phases.
